betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Accelerator Electromagnet Yoke§f

§aThe Electromagnet Yoke is the iron core of an Accelerator Electromagnet.§f
The §bAccelerator Electromagnet Yoke§f (`qmd:accelerator_yoke`, item `qmd:accelerator_yoke:0`) is a structural component placed inside §bLinear Accelerator (LINAC)§f and §bSynchrotron Ring Accelerator§f multiblocks. Each Yoke forms the iron core of an §bAccelerator Electromagnet§f, providing the magnetic field used to focus and steer the particle beam.

§6Crafting Recipe§f:
Per the §bminecraft.crafting§f recipe (graph category `minecraft.crafting`):
- Input 1: §bIron Ingot§f (`minecraft:iron_ingot`)
- Input 2: §bBioplastic§f (`nuclearcraft:part:6`, used as an insulator/former)
- Output: §bAccelerator Electromagnet Yoke§f (`qmd:accelerator_yoke`)

§6Use in Multiblocks§f:
- Yokes are placed as the §binner ring§f of an accelerator multiblock, surrounding the beam axis.
- Combined with §bAccelerator Coil§f wire and §bMagnet§f components, the Yoke + Coil form a complete electromagnet that contributes to the accelerator's §bquadrupoleStrength§f.
- The §bEquations.focusGain(quadrupoleStrength, stack)§f formula (per `Equations.java:29`) sets how much focus the electromagnet restores per pass: `gain = quadrupoleStrength * |charge|`.

§6Multiblock Role§f:
- In a §bLINAC§f, yokes are spaced periodically along the beam axis (one per RF cavity section).
- In a §bSynchrotron§f, yokes fill the corners and straight sections of the ring, working with §bDipole Magnets§f at the corners and §bQuadrupole Magnets§f on the straights.
- Yokes do §cnot§f produce heat themselves, but they §cincrease§f the multiblock's §bpower consumption§f (`part.getPower()`) and §bcooling requirement§f.

§eTip§f: Build your Yoke production line from an §bNC Manufactory§f fed by iron ingots and §bBioplastic§f. Bioplastic is crafted from sugar and §bBioplast§f sheet via the §bNC Manufactory§f — see the §bNC Bioplastic§f quest for the recipe chain.
